text:
There once was a girl named Emily who loved feathers. Every year she would collect the finest ones she could find. One year she got a large snowy owl.

One day Emily went to the owl's cage. The owl saw her and spread his wings. Emily put her hand out. He didn’t touch her.

She took a leap. He flew around her and landed on her head. She laughed.

That winter the owl flew south. Emily never saw him again.

She went home and drew pictures of him every day until she died. That's why birds flap their wings like wings.

That's just how she liked them.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
